source_pr.title: *: remove retool from Makefile
source_pr.body: ### What problem does this PR solve? Remove retool, as I've promised in https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/8755 `go.mod` could be used to control the version of the tools. A benefit of this change is that the module cache is available even in the CI environment, so unlike retool, it doesn't need to download files from network every time. And as @gregwebs has pointed out, the tool chains for our repo is complex and we should reconsider the retool approach. This commit make the tools management more uniform. ### What is changed and how it works? Make a _tools directory, touch the `go.mod` file, go get the binaries. update the Makefile remove unused file ### Check List Tests - No code --- This change is [](https://reviewable.io/reviews/pingcap/tidb/8769)

evidence: files: .gitignore, Makefile, _tools/go.mod, _tools/go.sum, circle.yml, errcheck_excludes.txt, go.mod, go.sum, hack/clean_vendor.sh, hack/hack_test.go, hack/retool-install.sh, tools.json, tools/check/check-tidy.sh, tools/check/check_parser_replace.sh, tools/check/go.mod, tools/check/go.sum, tools/check/revive.toml
